thesis: Applied Optoelectronics is a vertically integrated optical transceiver manufacturer riding the AI data center boom. Five consecutive quarters of record revenue, a $1.1B+ 2026 revenue guidance, and strong positioning in 800G/1.6T products create a compelling growth story. However, the stock has risen ~1,000% from lows and trades far above analyst consensus targets, creating significant valuation risk. The company is near an inflection point to profitability with $449M cash post-equity raise, but customer concentration and second-tier competitive position remain key risks.

drivers: 800G revenue reaches $217M/month by mid-2027 as projected
1.6T transceivers gain early market share with hyperscalers
Additional customer wins beyond current hyperscaler relationships
Operating leverage drives non-GAAP margins above 30%

risks: Hyperscaler AI capex cycle peaks and demand slows
Execution delays or yield issues on new products
Stock multiple compresses to match analyst consensus

base_case.assumptions: 800G ramps successfully through 2026-2027, 1.6T begins contributing in 2028, revenue reaches $1.4-1.6B by 2027 with non-GAAP operating margins of 12-15%. Stock trades at 15-20x forward earnings, consistent with optical peer multiples.

catalysts.event: Q2 2026 Earnings: Revenue guidance $180-198M; 800G mix update
Q3 2026 Earnings: Progress toward $1.1B annual run-rate
1.6T transceiver sampling and customer qualification announcements

key_risks: Second-tier competitive position vs. Coherent, Lumentum, Fabrinet
Extreme valuation disconnect: stock trades far above analyst consensus
Debt maturities within one year despite cash raise
China manufacturing exposure and geopolitical risk
AI capex cycle could slow in 2026-2027
